title: The Gnat and the Bull
lines: A Gnat alighted on the horn of a Bull.
After resting for some time, he said, "I hope you do not mind my sitting here; I do not intend to inconvenience you in the slightest."
"Oh, I had not even noticed you," replied the Bull.
The Gnat flew away in a rage.
When he returned after an absence, he asked: "Did you notice when I went away?"
moral: Small creatures may have great vanity.
